key_insight: PI-MFA reconstructs flow fields with B-splines while explicitly enforcing PDE residuals and boundary/initial conditions, so the continuous approximation stays physically consistent.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a compact, differentiable surrogate for flow data that is better suited for downstream analysis, visualization, and derivative-based quantities than purely data-driven interpolation.
method: Build a tensor-product B-spline representation with local support and exact analytical derivatives.
Solve for spline control points by balancing data fidelity against PDE residuals plus initial/boundary-condition penalties.
Use the resulting continuous field for efficient residual evaluation without storing full-resolution data.
evidence_signal: Across 1D convection-diffusion, 2D coupled Burgers, and 2D incompressible Navier-Stokes tests, PI-MFA reduced PDE residuals and improved balance-law consistency, with lower approximation error on physically inconsistent data than standard or regularized MFA.
watch_item: Performance depends on choosing appropriate PDE constraints and optimization weights; overly strong or mismatched physics terms could bias the reconstruction when the governing model is imperfect.
paper_title: A Physics-Informed B-Spline Framework for Continuous Approximation of Flow Data
